channel.title: Yuzu.solutions Insights
channel.description: BI news, workflows, and new technologies from the Yuzu press room.

item.title: Real-time analytics in Fabric: Eventstream, KQL, and the BI layer
item.description: How streaming events land in Eventhouse, when to expose them in Power BI vs Real-Time dashboards, and governance for high-velocity data.

item.title: Power BI deployment pipelines in practice: what to automate first
item.description: Dev, Test, Prod rules of thumb, dataset rules vs report-only deploys, and where GitHub Actions still beat native pipeline buttons.

item.title: Semantic model RLS at scale: patterns that survive reorgs
item.description: Dynamic RLS with Entra groups, bridge tables for many-to-many, and testing RLS before every deployment pipeline stage.

item.title: Fabric workspace Git: branching patterns BI teams actually use
item.description: Trunk-based vs feature branches, what to commit from Fabric, and how to avoid merge conflicts on semantic models and reports.

item.title: Direct Lake vs Import mode: when each storage mode earns its keep
item.description: Direct Lake cuts duplicate data and refresh windows. Import still wins for complex DAX and small datasets. A practical decision guide for Fabric semantic models.

item.title: Microsoft Fabric Copilot: first workflows to automate
item.description: Practical Copilot patterns for BI teams: measure scaffolding, pipeline documentation, and natural-language queries without breaking governance.

item.title: Power BI small wins from the 2026 rollout wave
item.description: Org apps with audiences, date picker slicers, DAX user-defined functions, report landing pages, Copilot on mobile, and PBIR default-on: practical picks from April–June 2026 Power BI updates.

item.title: Fabric Apps vs Power BI Apps: web apps vs report bundles
item.description: Fabric Apps are read/write web experiences on your lakehouse data. Power BI Apps are curated read-only report collections. Here is how to choose.

item.title: Why BI teams should treat Git like product code
item.description: Semantic models, reports, and pipelines belong in version control with PR reviews, CI checks, and deployment pipelines, not in shared folders.
